ref: Ham entered the tent and saw him naked, then went back outside and told his brothers.
text: Na Kanaan agya Ham kɔhunuu n’agya adagya mu, na ɔkɔka kyerɛɛ ne nuanom baanu no wɔ adihɔ.
translated: And Canaan saw Ham uncovering his father's nakedness, and he told his brothers about it outside.

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: Na ɔhyɛɛ deɛ ɔtɔ so mmienu ne deɛ ɔtɔ so mmiɛnsa ne wɔn a wɔdi mmoakuo no akyire nyinaa nso sɛ: Sɛ mokɔhunu Esau a, monka asɛm korɔ yi ara nkyerɛ no.
translated: And he explained the first thing he did and the second thing he did, and said to all who completely followed the advice: "If you meet Esau, do not speak this matter to him."

ref: While Jacob was kissing him, Isaac caught the smell of his clothes and said: “The smell of my son is like a field the Lord has blessed.
text: Na ɔtwe pinii kɔfee n’ano. Na Isak huaa n’atadeɛ mu hwa hwɛe, na ɔhyiraa no sɛ: Hwɛ, me ba ho hwa te sɛ afuo a AWURADE ahyira soɔ mu hwa.
translated: And he poured some coffee into his mouth. And Isaac, when he saw in his heart, said to him: Look, my son's voice is like the voice of Jacob whom the Lord blessed.

ref: and wandering through the fields, when a man asked, “What are you looking for?”
text: Na ɔbarima bi hunuu no, na hwɛ, na ɔrewɔ wira no mu ntwɛtwɛdɛ. Na ɔbarima no bisaa no sɛ: Worehwehwɛ deɛn?
translated: A man saw him, looked again, and saw that he was tied up in the rope. The man then asked him, "What are you staring at?"

ref: God said, “I command light to shine!” And light started shining.
text: Na Onyankopɔn kaa sɛ: Ɛnyɛ hann! Na ɛyɛɛ hann.
translated: And God said: It is not good! And it was good.
